sections.paragraph:
εὐϑύς, εῖα, ύ, gen. έως [origin unknown] in NT always applied to a term signifying a route of passage

—1. straight, as name of a street ἡ ῥύμη ϰαλουμένη Ἐυϑεῖα the street called ‘Straight’ (Street) Ac 9:11. In imagery of a path leveled for a digni-tary Mt 3:3 and par.; cp. Ac 13:10.

—2. of moral correctness straight, i.e. without deviation and therefore right 2 Pt 2:15.

εὐθύς [cp. prec.; = εὐθέως] adv. immediately, at once Mt 3:16; 13:20f; 14:27; 21:3; Mk 1:10 and oft. in Mk, freq. with apparent interest in energizing the narrative, e.g. 1:21, 23, 29 (in which some see a weakening to then, so then); Lk 6:49; J 13:30, 32: 19:34; Ac 10:16.
